Two branches inside a repeated alternati: Two branches inside a repeated alternation can consume the same text. When something later fails, the engine retries both the branch choices and the iteration boundaries; an unbounded repeat can make that growth exponential.
Two neighboring repeats can consume the: Two neighboring repeats can consume the same characters, so their boundary can move across the input. When something later fails, the engine retries every split, producing quadratic backtracking.
